message:
fix(tests): add mut to mpsc receivers and gate unused import behind feature flag

egress_tap_integration: Add mut qualifier to all mpsc::channel receiver
bindings (rx → mut rx) to satisfy the borrow checker when calling
recv().await, which requires &mut self on the receiver.

proptest_workflows_handlers: Gate the PathBuf import behind
#[cfg(feature = "browser")] since it is only used within browser-feature
test code, eliminating an unused-import warning in non-browser builds.

Also syncs Cargo.lock and beads issue tracker.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-e34d9.10.7.3","title":"[asupersync][ops] Runtime SLOs, alerts, and automated gate policies","description":"Define runtime-specific SLOs and implement automated alert/gate policies for regression prevention.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. SLOs cover cancellation latency, queue backlog, task leak rate, and service recovery time.\n2. Alert policies map to concrete failure classes and escalation paths.\n3. Unit tests validate SLO threshold and policy evaluation logic.\n4. Integration tests verify gate behavior in representative workload conditions.\n5. E2E scripts exercise threshold breaches and confirm alert/gate evidence logging.","notes":"SLOs should be tight enough to catch regressions early but stable enough for actionable signal. Heavy compile/test/benchmark/soak workloads for this bead must run via rch exec -- <command>, with reproducible command records and artifact paths captured in completion evidence. Require deterministic fixtures/seeds and replayable test evidence so failures are reproducible across environments. Require detailed structured logging/diagnostics with correlation_id, scenario_id, workflow_id, and failure taxonomy fields for every validation and recovery path. All diagnostics/logging/artifact outputs in this bead must comply with secret-safe redaction and privacy policy gates defined in ft-e34d9.10.7.5, with evidence captured in validation artifacts.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:26:52.565547Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-12T06:36:00.664821Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T06:36:00.664821Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","observability","quality-gate","sequencing","slo","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.7.3","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.7.3","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.7.3","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.7.3","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.7.3","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2313,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.7.3","author":"PinkForge","text":"Implementation exists in runtime_slo_gates.rs (956 lines, 30+ tests). All acceptance criteria met: SLOs for cancellation latency, queue backlog, task leak rate, recovery time + alert policies + gate evaluation. Closing as complete.","created_at":"2026-03-12T06:36:00Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-e34d9.10.7.4","title":"[asupersync][ops] User-facing diagnostics and remediation UX certification","description":"Certify that runtime diagnostics are actionable for both human CLI users and robot-mode agents, with precise remediation guidance and machine-parseable hints.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Define canonical diagnostic/remediation contract for top runtime failure classes (cause, impact, next-step command, escalation hint).\n2. Ensure outputs are dual-mode: concise/actionable for humans and machine-parseable hints for robot mode.\n3. Unit tests validate formatter correctness, hint generation, and message-stability snapshots.\n4. Integration tests inject representative runtime faults and assert remediation guidance remains accurate.\n5. E2E incident scripts verify that scripted recovery succeeds by following emitted guidance; logs include correlation IDs and trace links.\n6. Heavy incident-suite runs are executed via rch exec -- ... with reproducible evidence paths.","notes":"Strong runtime internals still fail users if diagnostics are vague. This bead converts observability data into recoverability for real workflows. Require deterministic fixtures/seeds and replayable test evidence so failures are reproducible across environments. All diagnostics/logging/artifact outputs in this bead must comply with secret-safe redaction and privacy policy gates defined in ft-e34d9.10.7.5, with evidence captured in validation artifacts.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:47:15.393453Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-12T06:36:00.665136Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T06:36:00.665136Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","doctor","e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","observability","quality-gate","sequencing","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.7.4","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.7.4","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.6.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.7.4","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.7.4","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.7.4","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.7.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.7.4","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.7.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2314,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.7.4","author":"PinkForge","text":"Implementation exists in runtime_diagnostics_ux.rs (866 lines, 20 tests). All acceptance criteria met: diagnostic templates for all 10 failure classes, dual-mode rendering (human/robot), certification report, remediation catalog. Closing as complete.","created_at":"2026-03-12T06:36:00Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-e34d9.10.7.5","title":"[asupersync][ops] Secret-safe diagnostics: redaction, privacy, and artifact hygiene gate","description":"Ensure all runtime diagnostics, telemetry, and evidence artifacts preserve triage utility while preventing leakage of secrets, credentials, tokens, and sensitive user content across CLI/robot/watch/worker surfaces.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Deliver full scope for this bead without regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests for happy path, boundary/edge behavior, and explicit failure/error handling.
3. Add deterministic integration/end-to-end scenarios including at least one failure injection and one recovery path.
4. Emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions, outcomes, reason_code/error_code) and persist triage artifacts.
5. Document run commands and evidence locations and wire them into normal validation/CI paths.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Define a versioned sensitive-data taxonomy and redaction policy covering credentials, tokens, secrets, PII-like fields, and high-risk payload fragments across runtime logs/artifacts.\n2. Implement deterministic redaction/scrubbing rules for structured logs, incident bundles, evidence manifests, and test artifacts while preserving correlation and triage value.\n3. Add comprehensive unit tests for redaction engines (positive and adversarial cases), including no-leak assertions, false-positive bounds, and deterministic output guarantees.\n4. Add integration tests across CLI, robot mode, watch daemon, and health/doctor outputs to verify sensitive fields are redacted consistently and safely.\n5. Add end-to-end scripts that inject synthetic secrets/tokens into realistic workflows and prove they never appear unredacted in logs, traces, manifests, or exported evidence bundles.\n6. Structured diagnostics must include explicit redaction metadata (redaction_rule_id, field_class, hash/fingerprint where appropriate, correlation_id) for auditability without exposing raw secrets.\n7. Heavy validation suites run via rch exec -- <command>; closure requires artifact-level evidence proving zero unredacted secret leakage in gated scenarios.","notes":"diagnostic_redaction.rs: 3-layer redaction (secret-pattern, field-policy, privacy-budget). 27 unit tests pass, clippy clean. Commit dd53b606.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:39:10.084229Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-26T08:54:43.994166Z","closed_at":"2026-02-26T08:54:43.994114Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["artifacts","asupersync","e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","observability","privacy","quality-gate","security","sequencing","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.7.5","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.6.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.7.5","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.7.5","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.7.5","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"ft-e34d9.10.8","title":"[asupersync][track] Dependency eradication and cutover governance","description":"Finish migration by removing forbidden runtime dependencies and executing controlled cutover.

Scope:
- dependency eradication (tokio/smol/async-io residues)
- compile-time/runtime guards against regression
- canary rollout, rollback rehearsal, and final go/no-go

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Remove direct forbidden runtime dependencies from target crates per migration boundary.
2. Add static guards to prevent runtime dependency reintroduction.
3. Execute canary and rollback rehearsals with explicit success criteria.
4. Final go/no-go requires green unit/integration/e2e suites and operational evidence bundle.
5. Closure includes self-contained migration report capturing decisions, tradeoffs, and residual risks.","notes":"This track prevents migration backsliding and guarantees operationally safe landing. The bar is evidence-based closure, not partial code conversion, and every gate must include structured logging/diagnostics artifacts for auditability. Final acceptance is user-centered: CLI/robot/watch/session workflows must show stable latency, reliability, and recovery behavior under cutover conditions. Heavy compile/test/benchmark/soak workloads for this bead must run via rch exec -- <command>, with reproducible command records and artifact paths captured in completion evidence. Require deterministic fixtures/seeds and replayable test evidence so failures are reproducible across environments. Include failure-injection and degraded-path scenarios (timeouts/cancellation/error classes) with explicit assertions and diagnostics. This bead must include comprehensive unit tests for scope-local logic, edge cases, and failure branches with deterministic fixtures and replayable seeds. This bead must include cross-module integration tests that validate boundary behavior, data contracts, and degraded/failure interactions under representative workloads. All diagnostics/logging/artifact outputs in this bead must comply with secret-safe redaction and privacy policy gates defined in ft-e34d9.10.7.5, with evidence captured in validation artifacts.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:24:39.230023Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-21T09:55:42.895426Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","cleanup","cutover","e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","quality-gate","sequencing","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.8","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.8","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.8","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.8","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2168,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.8","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"PinkForge session 15: picking up. Creating dependency_eradication.rs with compile-time guards, forbidden import scanners, and migration report builder.","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:08:37Z"},{"id":2428,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.8","author":"LavenderRaven","text":"Taking a bounded support slice on 2026-03-21 in crates/frankenterm-gui/src/overlay/debug.rs and crates/frankenterm-gui/src/overlay/copy.rs only. Scope: remove remaining direct smol runtime/timer usage on these GUI overlay surfaces in favor of existing promise::spawn runtime-agnostic helpers (block_on/sleep), with focused local tests where appropriate. Avoiding render/web.rs/mux-client lanes; heavy cargo validation, if reached, will run only via rch exec -- ... per repo policy.","created_at":"2026-03-21T09:10:58Z"},{"id":2429,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.8","author":"jemanuel","text":"Completed a bounded support slice in crates/frankenterm-gui/src/overlay/debug.rs and crates/frankenterm-gui/src/overlay/copy.rs on 2026-03-21. Replaced direct smol runtime/timer usage with existing promise::spawn helpers: smol::block_on -> promise::spawn::block_on in overlay/debug.rs, and smol::Timer::after(Duration::from_millis(350)) -> promise::spawn::sleep(Duration::from_millis(350)) in overlay/copy.rs. Verified text-level residue removal with rg and formatting with rustfmt. Remote validation was attempted only via rch exec -- cargo check/clippy for frankenterm-gui, but both runs are currently blocked by worker environment issue RCH-E307: missing x11-xcb pkg-config metadata (x11-xcb.pc) on the remote worker, so no source-level cargo result is available from this slice. br update remains locally blocked by the existing blocked_issues_cache.issue_id uniqueness failure, so status tracking for this slice is recorded here in comments instead.","created_at":"2026-03-21T09:18:08Z"},{"id":2430,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.8","author":"jemanuel","text":"Starting a second bounded support slice on 2026-03-21 in crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/mod.rs, crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/keyevent.rs, and crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/render/paint.rs only. Scope: replace direct smol::Timer::at(...) usage inside existing promise::spawn tasks with promise::spawn::sleep(target.saturating_duration_since(Instant::now())) so these GUI termwindow surfaces stop depending directly on smol timers. Avoiding scripting/guiwin.rs channel migration and all active mux/runtime/web/core files.","created_at":"2026-03-21T09:19:55Z"},{"id":2432,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.8","author":"jemanuel","text":"Completed the second bounded GUI termwindow support slice on 2026-03-21 in crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/mod.rs, crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/keyevent.rs, and crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/render/paint.rs only. Replaced direct smol::Timer::at(...) usage inside promise::spawn tasks with promise::spawn::sleep(target.saturating_duration_since(Instant::now())) and removed the corresponding direct smol timer imports. Lightweight local verification passed: git diff --check clean, rustfmt --check clean, and rg confirms no remaining smol::Timer / Timer::at residues in those three files. Heavy validation was attempted only via rch exec -- cargo check/clippy for frankenterm-gui. Both remote runs progressed through substantial compilation and then failed with the same worker environment issue: RCH-E307 missing system package metadata for crate x11 (x11.pc / pkg-config x11). No local cargo fallback was used.","created_at":"2026-03-21T09:25:20Z"},{"id":2433,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.8","author":"jemanuel","text":"Starting a third bounded GUI support slice on 2026-03-21 in crates/frankenterm-gui/src/scripting/guiwin.rs, crates/frankenterm-gui/src/overlay/debug.rs, crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/mod.rs, and crates/frankenterm-gui/Cargo.toml only. Scope: replace the remaining direct smol::channel one-shot request/reply paths with flume bounded channels (Sender/Receiver/TrySendError plus recv_async/send_async) so frankenterm-gui no longer depends directly on smol in source. Avoiding broader config/mux/core/vendored async migrations.","created_at":"2026-03-21T09:31:09Z"},{"id":2434,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.8","author":"jemanuel","text":"Starting a fresh bounded support slice on 2026-03-21 in frankenterm/config/src/lib.rs and frankenterm/config/Cargo.toml only. Scope: migrate the LuaPipe config-reload handoff from smol::channel::unbounded to flume::unbounded, remove the now-unneeded smol import/prelude usage in config lib, and swap the crate dependency from smol to flume. Avoiding the dirty core mux/client wrapper lanes and the already-converged GUI flume slice.","created_at":"2026-03-21T09:42:12Z"},{"id":2435,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.8","author":"jemanuel","text":"LavenderRaven: bounded config LuaPipe flume slice converged externally before landing; both frankenterm/config/src/lib.rs and frankenterm/config/Cargo.toml now match HEAD with no local diff. Verified current HEAD state with remote-only rch cargo check -p config --all-targets on worker vmi1152480 (remote cargo exit=0); note that config crate still emits pre-existing warnings under plain check, and the long post-build rsync artifact retrieval was interrupted after remote success to avoid unnecessary local transfer churn.","created_at":"2026-03-21T09:53:25Z"},{"id":2436,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.8","author":"jemanuel","text":"LavenderRaven: starting bounded vendored-window support slice on 2026-03-21 in frankenterm/window/Cargo.toml and frankenterm/window/src/os/wayland/window.rs only. Scope: replace the Wayland first-configure async_channel handshake with flume and remove direct async-channel/async-io deps from the window crate if the source remains clean. Avoiding promise spawn internals and all other platform/event-loop files.","created_at":"2026-03-21T09:55:42Z"}]}

Scope: replace the remaining direct smol::channel one-shot request/reply paths with flume bounded channels (Sender/Receiver/TrySendError plus recv_async/send_async) so frankenterm-gui no longer depends directly on smol in source. Avoiding broader config/mux/core/vendored async migrations.","created_at":"2026-03-21T09:31:09Z"},{"id":2434,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.8","author":"jemanuel","text":"Starting a fresh bounded support slice on 2026-03-21 in frankenterm/config/src/lib.rs and frankenterm/config/Cargo.toml only. Scope: migrate the LuaPipe config-reload handoff from smol::channel::unbounded to flume::unbounded, remove the now-unneeded smol import/prelude usage in config lib, and swap the crate dependency from smol to flume. Avoiding the dirty core mux/client wrapper lanes and the already-converged GUI flume slice.","created_at":"2026-03-21T09:42:12Z"},{"id":2435,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.8","author":"jemanuel","text":"LavenderRaven: bounded config LuaPipe flume slice converged externally before landing; both frankenterm/config/src/lib.rs and frankenterm/config/Cargo.toml now match HEAD with no local diff. Scope: replace the Wayland first-configure async_channel handshake with flume and remove direct async-channel/async-io deps from the window crate if the source remains clean. Avoiding promise spawn internals and all other platform/event-loop files.","created_at":"2026-03-21T09:55:42Z"},{"id":2437,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.8","author":"jemanuel","text":"LavenderRaven: completed bounded vendored-window support slice on 2026-03-21 in frankenterm/window/Cargo.toml and frankenterm/window/src/os/wayland/window.rs only. Replaced the Wayland first-configure one-shot from async_channel::bounded(1) to flume::bounded(1) with Sender<()> + recv_async()/try_send(), and removed the window crate’s direct async-channel/async-io dependencies from Cargo.toml. Local hygiene passed: rustfmt --edition 2024 --check on the touched wayland file, git diff --check clean, and rg shows no remaining async_channel:: / async-io residue in those two files or under frankenterm/window/src. Remote-only validation via rch stayed offloaded for both cargo check -p window --all-targets and cargo clippy -p window --all-targets -- -D warnings, but both failed on worker vmi1227854 with RCH-E307 missing x11.pc / system x11 pkg-config metadata, so no source-level cargo verdict is available from the worker environment.","created_at":"2026-03-21T09:58:35Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-e34d9.10.8.1","title":"[asupersync][cutover] Runtime dependency eradication (tokio/smol/async-io)","description":"Remove remaining forbidden runtime dependencies in scoped crates and align manifests/features with target architecture.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Remove direct forbidden runtime deps from scoped manifests or isolate behind explicit non-default compatibility gates.\n2. Validate dependency graph is coherent and minimal after cleanup.\n3. Unit tests validate feature-flag behavior and compatibility boundaries.\n4. Integration tests ensure cleaned dependency graph still supports required workflows.\n5. E2E scripts validate runtime behavior in cleaned build profiles with structured logs.","notes":"Dependency cleanup is a correctness and maintainability requirement, not just aesthetic debt work. Include explicit rollback/fallback/recovery validation criteria with pass/fail outcomes for user-facing workflows. Require deterministic fixtures/seeds and replayable test evidence so failures are reproducible across environments. Include failure-injection and degraded-path scenarios (timeouts/cancellation/error classes) with explicit assertions and diagnostics. All diagnostics/logging/artifact outputs in this bead must comply with secret-safe redaction and privacy policy gates defined in ft-e34d9.10.7.5, with evidence captured in validation artifacts. Heavy compile/test/benchmark/soak workloads for this bead must run via rch exec -- <command>, with reproducible command records and artifact paths captured in completion evidence.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BronzeHarbor","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:26:53.365911Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-12T06:42:13.978506Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T06:42:13.978506Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","cleanup","dependencies","e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","quality-gate","sequencing","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.8.1","depends_on_id":"ft-2h1ha","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.8.1","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.8.1","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.8.1","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2315,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.8.1","author":"PinkForge","text":"Implementation in dependency_eradication.rs + web route fix (4b7b7e41). Was previously closed (db15133e). Re-closing.","created_at":"2026-03-12T06:42:13Z"}]}

 {"id":"ft-e34d9.10.8.3","title":"[asupersync][cutover] Canary rollout, rollback rehearsals, and fail-safe drills","description":"Execute staged canary rollout and rollback rehearsals for runtime-migrated paths under controlled fault conditions.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Define canary cohorts, promotion criteria, and rollback triggers for runtime cutover.\n2. Run rollback rehearsals that prove fast, safe recovery from failed rollout states.\n3. Unit tests validate rollout state-machine helpers and policy gates.\n4. Integration tests exercise promotion/rollback transitions in automation.\n5. E2E scripts run canary+rollback drills with exhaustive structured logs and artifacts.\n6. Enforce user-disruption budgets (latency/error/recovery thresholds) during canary promotion decisions.\n7. Heavy rehearsal and soak-like runs are executed via rch exec -- ... with reproducible evidence capture.","notes":"Rollout safety must be practiced, not assumed. Treat rehearsal evidence and user-impact budgets as release prerequisites. Require deterministic fixtures/seeds and replayable test evidence so failures are reproducible across environments. All diagnostics/logging/artifact outputs in this bead must comply with secret-safe redaction and privacy policy gates defined in ft-e34d9.10.7.5, with evidence captured in validation artifacts.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:26:55.410847Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-12T06:36:34.743401Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T06:36:34.743401Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","cutover","e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","quality-gate","rollout","sequencing","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.8.3","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.8.3","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.7.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.8.3","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.8.3","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2316,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.8.3","author":"PinkForge","text":"Implementation exists in canary_rehearsal.rs (1818 lines, 39 tests).
